title: NFL Rules Demo Corpus
notes: Static internal-demo rule corpus for bounded NFL Analyze retrieval.
Summaries are operator prompts, not legal advice or a complete CBA parser.
Use source URLs and live club/NFL/NFLPA guidance before external decisions.

rules.title: Salary-to-signing-bonus restructure
rules.summary: NFL base salary can be converted into signing bonus for cap purposes; the converted amount is prorated over the remaining contract years, creating current-year room and future cap charges.
rules.analysis_use: Use for current-year cap-room questions where a veteran contract has enough remaining years and cash to convert.
rules.source_note: NFL CBA public reference; verify article/section and club-specific contract terms before external use.

rules.title: Post-June 1 release or trade accounting
rules.summary: Post-June 1 treatment generally keeps current-year bonus proration in the current season and pushes remaining acceleration into the following league year.
rules.analysis_use: Use for cut/trade timing comparisons where dead money and immediate room differ by transaction date.
rules.source_note: NFL CBA public reference; verify designation limits and player-specific bonus schedules.

rules.title: Franchise and transition tenders
rules.summary: A club may use one franchise or transition tag in the tag window; the tender creates a cap charge and player-specific negotiation leverage.
rules.analysis_use: Use for pending free agents where the club is weighing tag, extension, or walk-away paths.
rules.source_note: NFL CBA public reference; tag windows and tender amounts require current league-year confirmation.

rules.title: Rookie deals and fifth-year options
rules.summary: Drafted rookie contracts and fifth-year-option decisions shape extension timing, tag leverage, and cap planning for core players.
rules.analysis_use: Use for first-round rookie planning, extension timing, and tag-leverage questions.
rules.source_note: NFL CBA public reference; option tiers and deadlines require current league-year confirmation.

rules.title: Practice squad and roster churn
rules.summary: Practice-squad rules create a lower-cost development and emergency-depth lane, but active-roster elevation and signing limits constrain repeated use.
rules.analysis_use: Use for fringe roster construction and short-term injury-depth planning.
rules.source_note: NFL football operations public explainer; verify current league-year roster limits.

rules.title: IR, PUP, and NFI availability lists
rules.summary: Injury and availability lists affect roster spots, activation timing, and cap/contract planning but do not replace player-specific medical and contract review.
rules.analysis_use: Use for availability caveats when roster and cap plans depend on injured players.
rules.source_note: NFL public rulebook/operations reference; verify CBA and league memo details.

rules.title: Waiver claims and vested veterans
rules.summary: Waiver status affects whether a released player can be claimed before becoming a free agent, which changes cut, stash, and re-sign planning.
rules.analysis_use: Use for young/depth-player release and reacquisition scenarios.
rules.source_note: NFL public rulebook/operations reference; verify current waiver calendar and player service time.

rules.title: Compensatory pick formula posture
rules.summary: Compensatory pick outcomes depend on qualifying free-agent losses and gains, contract value, playing time, and postseason honors, so team posture should be treated as directional until awards are official.
rules.analysis_use: Use for free-agent walk/sign tradeoff caveats.
rules.source_note: NFL operations public reference; formula details are not fully public.

rules.title: NFL player and pick trades
rules.summary: NFL trades require league approval and carry cap-accounting consequences for bonus acceleration, acquiring-team salary obligations, roster limits, and draft-pick ownership.
rules.analysis_use: Use for veteran contract movement and draft-capital planning.
rules.source_note: NFL public rulebook/operations reference plus CBA cap-accounting concepts.

rules.title: Team salary cap accounting
rules.summary: NFL cap planning combines salary, prorated bonuses, dead money, incentives, rollover, top-51/offseason treatment, and transaction timing.
rules.analysis_use: Use as the umbrella caveat for cap-room, cut, restructure, tag, and extension analysis.
rules.source_note: NFL CBA public reference; club cap sheets remain the row-level source of truth.

rules.title: Contract extensions
rules.summary: Extensions can lower or raise current-year cap through new money, signing bonus proration, guarantees, option bonuses, void years, and future cash commitments.
rules.analysis_use: Use for extend-versus-restructure questions and core-player timing decisions.
rules.source_note: NFL CBA public reference; player-specific deal terms are required for exact modeling.
